title: Decouple Bureau statements from ambient checkout state
goal: Keep Bureau statements available through a deployment-bound, hash-verified, read-only Registry snapshot even when the conventional Bureau checkout is stale or dirty, while requiring every Registry mutation to bind an explicit clean current task worktree.

acceptance.assertion: Every installed Bureau release creates an immutable tracked-file Registry snapshot whose inventory binds source commit, path set, inventory SHA-256 and complete tree SHA-256.

The canonical launcher selects that snapshot by manifest-bound environment instead of the caller's working directory, so statements remain valid when ~/repos/bureau is stale, dirty or used by another process.
Commands explicitly classified as statements use a query-only StateStore path that performs no directory creation, migration, journal-mode change or chmod effect.

A mutating command against the canonical snapshot fails with explicit-registry-root-required and can proceed only when --root names a clean Bureau task worktree whose head matches the deployed source commit.
Missing, malformed, symlinked, source-mismatched or content-drifted snapshots fail closed before Registry truth or mutation authority is claimed.
No path automatically pulls, resets, stashes, cleans, overwrites or removes the conventional main checkout or any foreign worktree.

Focused and full tests cover root precedence, dirty unrelated cwd, read-only SQLite, explicit clean override, blocked implicit mutation and snapshot tampering; live proof repeats the original stale-dirty-main scenario after deployment.

observation.failure: The current immutable runtime correctly refused the stale dirty checkout, but because the launcher defaulted Registry root to the caller cwd it also refused every normal statement.

metadata.safety_boundary: Availability is restored only for read truth. Mutation authority remains stricter than before and always requires an explicit clean task worktree.
